_note: Round-12 follow-up #5: multi-part text attrs on `<slot>` (e.g. `label="hi {name}"`) — pre-fix native dropped these attrs entirely from the slot literal, so consumer-side `let:label` typed as `any` and any field/argument check passed silently. Upstream's `slot.ts:46` casts multi-part interpolated attrs to a dummy string expression giving `label: string` at the slot prop type. Native now emits multi-part attrs as `Resolved(Type("string"))` slot attrs. Producer here exposes `label: string` from `<slot label="hi {name}" />`; Consumer's `takesString(label)` passes; the swapped `takesNumber(label)` correctly fires TS2345 (`string` not assignable to `number`). Pre-fix `label` was `any` and both calls would have passed silently.
